text.title: csharp-xunit-testing Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for csharp-xunit-testing. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every controller test class MUST use IClassFixture<WebApplicationFactory<Program>> and override ConfigureWebHost to substitute the DbContext with SQLite-in-memory. Never use the production database or real external APIs in controller tests.
rule.rationale: Tests must be deterministic and fast. Production databases are off-limits; external APIs are blocked by the network in CI. SQLite-in-memory gives you a real relational database without persistence.

rule.statement: Controller tests MUST follow AAA (Arrange-Act-Assert) layout with blank-line separators between phases. Assertions MUST use FluentAssertions (.Should().BeEquivalentTo(...)) for readable diffs on failure.
rule.rationale: AAA layout is scannable by both humans and agents. FluentAssertions produces multiline diffs that pinpoint which field failed, vs. xUnit's Assert.Equal which shows the entire object stringified.

rule.statement: Service unit tests MUST use [Fact] for single-input tests and [Theory] + [InlineData] for parametric boundary tests. Each test class MUST mock collaborators (repository, external service) via constructor injection or Moq.Of<T>().
rule.rationale: [Fact] is simpler for single cases; [Theory] avoids copy-paste of similar test logic. Mocking isolates the class-under-test from its dependencies.

rule.statement: Every mocked Setup MUST use argument matchers precisely (It.Is<T>(...) rather than It.IsAny<T>()) to catch accidental divergence between test setup and test code.
rule.rationale: Loose It.IsAny matchers hide bugs: a test passes even if the code calls the method with different arguments than expected. Precise matchers fail fast.

rule.statement: Never use static fields or caches in test code. If tests must be serialized (e.g., all use the same port), mark the class with [CollectionDefinition(DisableParallelization = true)].
rule.rationale: Static state persists across tests and causes flakes in parallel runs. Serialization is the hammer; use sparingly and document why.

rule.statement: For tests that share a WebApplicationFactory, use ICollectionFixture<TestAppFactory> and decorate the class with [Collection("Integration")]. This creates a single factory for all tests in the collection and runs them serially.
rule.rationale: Each WebApplicationFactory is a separate port and database. Sharing saves startup cost; serialization prevents port collisions.

rule.statement: For test fixture data, create a builder class or use AutoFixture to generate realistic default values. Never hardcode data literals inline unless the test is about that specific value.
rule.rationale: Builders are DRY and document the minimal required fields for a test case. AutoFixture generates plausible defaults (unique emails, valid UUIDs) that prevent false positives.

rule.statement: All assertions MUST use FluentAssertions (.Should().xxx) rather than xUnit's Assert.xxx. Assert.Equal on objects produces a single stringified line; Should().BeEquivalentTo produces a multiline diff.
rule.rationale: Multiline diffs pinpoint which field failed. Single-line stringified output is hard to debug for agents and humans.
